summary: Draft PR for menu main-rule decomp work. It added C bodies for mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714 in src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c, updated related prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in mnmainrule.h and mnmain.h, and scoped dont_inline pragmas around two existing mnmain.c helpers. The bot report credited one new full match, mn_802307F8, plus near-matches for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714. The PR was closed unmerged and has no human review comments in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source changes: added mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, mn_80231714 bodies, local GObjBitfield typedef, and extern declarations for menu globals/floats.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c hunks adding GObjBitfield, externs, mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, mn_80231714
text_corpus: bot report lists mn_802307F8 full match and mn_8023164C/mn_80231714 improvements

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for newly decompiled mnmainrule functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mn_802307F8 changed to void(s32,s32,s32); mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 changed to void(void)

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for neighboring mnmain functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mn_8022EBDC, mn_8022F268, mn_8022F360, mn_8022F4CC changed away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS

key_files.role: Removed old nonmatching markers for already present functions and added scoped dont_inline pragmas around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on/off added around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78; old /// # markers removed for mn_8022EA08, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022EB24, mn_8022EB78

key_files.role: Empty validation marker present in diff excerpt, but omitted from changed_files metadata.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: diff --git a/.build_validated b/.build_validated new file mode 100644
metadata_json changed_files: only four source/header files listed

what_changed.text: Implemented mn_802307F8 with cleanup of an existing SIS text object at offset 0x130, selection of a text/index byte from mn_804D4B96 or mn_803EC818, creation of an HSD_Text via HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, assignment to font_size.x/y, and HSD_SisLib_803A6368 call.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report marks mn_802307F8 0.00% to 100.00%, +248 bytes

what_changed.text: Implemented mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 as very similar menu-flow setup routines: update mn_804A04F0 prev/current menu, choose hovered_selection and mode via switch on previous menu, create/proc a GObj, set render_plink, then create/link another menu GObj via mn_80230E38; mn_80231714 additionally sets mn_804D6BD4 = 1.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists both functions as 99.90% improvements, not full matches

what_changed.text: Updated mnmainrule.h prototypes for the three implemented mnmainrule functions from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures.
Updated several mnmain.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void-return signatures.
Added scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 in mnmain.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/mn/mnmain.c hunk around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78

smart_moves.text: Header signatures were updated alongside recovered bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS surface instead of leaving stale prototypes.
The switch form in mn_8023164C/mn_80231714 expresses adjacent menu cases cleanly instead of preserving a pure branch ladder.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switch ((s32) data->prev_menu) in mn_8023164C and mn_80231714

smart_moves.text: The dont_inline pragmas were scoped with on/off pairs around individual functions rather than left as a broad file-level setting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on/off around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled body proves a signature, update the owning header in the same PR so callers no longer depend on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnmainrule.h and mnmain.h prototype changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Near-matches should remain distinguishable from full matches: the bot reported mn_802307F8 at 100.00%, while mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 were only 99.90%.
Temporary local structs or raw offset accesses can unblock a match, but they should be treated as type-recovery debt rather than final style when real fields are not established in the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local GObjBitfield typedef and casts
raw_diff: u8* data and accesses at offsets 2 and 0x130

naming_conventions.text: Address-style function and data names were preserved for unrecovered semantics, including mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, mn_80231714, mn_804D4B96, mn_803EC818, and mn_804DBE18..mn_804DBE2C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added function definitions and extern declarations

naming_conventions.text: Short local names such as data, prev, gobj, mode, sis, and zero were used for clear local roles; register-like r30 remained in mn_802307F8, suggesting incomplete semantic naming there.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on/off additions in mnmain.c
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The loaded evidence does not include an explanation or adjacent-function objdiff for why these pragmas were required; accepted standards require narrow proof for pragma exceptions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local bitfield overlay GObjBitfield used to write render_plink at the expected bit position on an HSD_GObj.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef GObjBitfield and ((GObjBitfield*) gobj)->render_plink = HSD_GObj_804D783C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Potentially a matching tactic or missing type evidence; PR slice does not prove this as the canonical HSD_GObj type shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Raw byte-pointer access used for mn_802307F8 input data, including offset 0x130 SIS pointer and byte offset 2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Useful for matching but should be revisited if a menu data struct is known or later recovered.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch-based menu case handling used for previous menu values 0x0F and 0x10 with a default mode.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switch in mn_8023164C and mn_80231714
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Consistent with standard advice to test canonical control-flow forms.

This is already broadly covered by avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics-need-evidence; curator may add this as an indexed example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote mn_8023164C or mn_80231714 as fully matched; the bot report says 99.90%, not 100.00%.
Do not infer that the PR was merged or accepted; metadata has state CLOSED and merged_at null.
Do not treat .build_validated alone as proof of a successful build; it is an empty file in the diff and the bot report is the only loaded validation-style evidence.
Do not promote GObjBitfield, mn_803EC818, or mn_804DBE18..mn_804DBE2C semantics beyond their observed use in this diff; names and types remain address-style or local.
